cards.goal: Send the first clear signal to your gut using 5 simple steps

cards.title: Before you start
paragraphs: This is a general wellness program, not medical treatment or a diagnosis.
If you have a diagnosed gut condition, recent abdominal surgery, a heart condition, or symptoms that concern you, consult your doctor before starting or modify the practice safely.
Do not start probiotic supplements during the program. Avoid cold drinks and avoid eating after 9 PM while following this reset.
cards.highlight: Use the program as daily support, not as a replacement for medical care.

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Most gut programs start by telling you what to eat differently. This one starts differently. Your gut has its own nervous system: 500 million nerve cells that respond to signals before food even arrives. Day 1 wakes up these nerve pathways with five simple steps. Nothing complicated. Nothing expensive. Everything free. You do not need to change what you eat today. Only the temperature of your drinks.

cards.title: Step 1: Cold Water Face Splash
instructions: Go to your sink. Splash cold water on your face 5 to 7 times.
Do this before tea, before food, before your phone.
Takes 10 seconds.
cards.whyThisWorks: Cold water on the face triggers the dive reflex: your body's ancient survival response. This immediately wakes up the vagus nerve which is the direct physical connection between your brain and your gut. When the vagus nerve fires, your gut starts moving food properly and digestive enzymes switch on.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: 2 to 4 days: less bloating, more regular mornings

cards.title: Step 2: Tongue Scraping
instructions: Before brushing your teeth, scrape your tongue from back to front 5 to 7 times.
Use a tongue scraper or the back edge of a metal spoon.
Look at what comes off: that coating is bacteria that built up overnight.
Then brush teeth normally.
cards.whyThisWorks: Your mouth and gut share the same system. Every morning without scraping, you swallow that overnight bacteria film with your first sip: and it travels straight to your gut. Removing it takes 30 seconds and directly reduces the harmful bacteria reaching your gut daily.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: 3 to 4 days: morning nausea reduces, appetite feels cleaner

cards.title: Step 3: 500 ml Warm Water on Waking
instructions: After tongue scraping, drink 500 ml of warm water slowly over 2 to 3 minutes.
Not hot. Not cold. Warm: roughly body temperature.
Important rule for today: no cold drinks for the rest of the day.
cards.whyThisWorks: Warm water triggers the gastrocolic reflex: your gut's natural signal to empty the bowel. Cold water tightens the gut muscle and disrupts this reflex. This single step resolves morning constipation for many people within 3 to 5 days.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: 3 to 5 days: more regular morning bowel movement

cards.title: Step 4: The Chewing Practice
instructions: Choose any one meal today.
Put your phone away. Sit down.
On the first 5 bites: chew each one 20 times before swallowing.
After 5 bites, eat the rest of the meal normally.
5 bites only today: that is all.
cards.whyThisWorks: Digestion begins in the mouth. Saliva contains a digestive enzyme called amylase that starts breaking down food before it reaches the stomach. When food arrives already partially broken down, the stomach does 30% less work. Less undigested food in the lower gut means less fermentation, less gas, and less bloating.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: 3 to 5 days: post-meal bloating reduces noticeably

cards.title: Step 5: Evening Humming
instructions: In the evening: in the shower, while cooking, or sitting quietly: hum any note for 2 minutes.
Keep the sound low and steady.
Feel the vibration in your chest and throat.
cards.whyThisWorks: Humming creates vibration in the throat that directly stimulates the vagus nerve. This sends a rest-and-digest signal to the entire digestive tract. 2 minutes of humming is essentially a gentle evening massage of your gut's nervous system.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: 2 to 4 days: less gut cramping in the evenings, better sleep

cards.title: Water Schedule for Today
instructions: On waking: 500 ml warm water before tea or food
30 minutes before breakfast: 200 ml warm water
30 minutes before lunch: 200 ml warm water
Mid-afternoon around 4 PM: 300 ml warm water or jeera water
30 minutes before dinner: 200 ml warm water
After dinner: 200 ml warm water (not during the meal)
Total target: 2.5 litres across the day
Rule: No cold water. Warm or room temperature only all day.
Rule: Only small sips during meals: not large glasses.

cards.title: Today's Gut Trick
instructions: The fastest way to settle an upset stomach right now: splash cold water on your face, then hum for 60 seconds. The vagus nerve activation calms gut spasms faster than most antacids. Try it the next time you feel uncomfortable after a meal.

cards.helperText: One sentence is enough. Notice what your gut taught you today.

cards.checklistQuote: Showing up honestly is the first act of healing.

cards.goal: Build the map of when, where and why your gut reacts

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: You cannot improve what you cannot see. Today you build your personal gut pattern map: the exact times, places, emotions, and foods that affect your digestion. Most people experience gut discomfort as random and unpredictable. It is not. It follows very clear patterns once you start looking. The more honest and specific your map, the more useful the rest of this program becomes.

cards.title: Step 1: Full Morning Protocol
instructions: Tongue scraping → Cold water face → 500 ml warm water
This three-step sequence is now your fixed morning gut start every day.
cards.whyThisWorks: Consistency builds the habit faster. Do it in the same order every morning.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: Building from Day 1

cards.title: Step 2: Build Your Pattern Map
instructions: Throughout today, notice and mentally note your answers to these four questions:
1. WHEN: what time of day does your gut feel worst? (morning, after lunch, evening)
2. WHAT FOOD: does discomfort follow any specific food? (dairy, wheat, oily food, raw vegetables)
3. WHAT EMOTION: what feeling was present when the gut reacted? (stress, anxiety, rushing)
4. WHAT SITUATION: after which activity? (big meal, skipping a meal, long sitting)
cards.whyThisWorks: Patterns are information. They tell you where the gut needs support most.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: 3 to 5 days: awareness of your triggers reduces anxiety about gut reactions

cards.title: Dairy is one of the most common hidden gut triggers in India
paragraphs: As you build your pattern map today, pay specific attention to dairy.
Note whether bloating, gas or discomfort appears 30 minutes to 2 hours after: milk in chai, a glass of milk, curd, paneer, ice cream, or packaged flavoured yogurt.
If you notice a pattern with dairy: write it down clearly. This is an important signal we will work with on Days 5, 9, and 16.
You do not need to remove dairy today: just observe and note.

cards.goal: Add the warm compress and jeera water: feel the difference after one meal

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: The gut works best at body temperature. Cold drinks shock it. Warmth supports it. Today adds the warm compress: a simple clinical technique used in physiotherapy for gut problems that almost nobody knows about in daily life. Most people feel the difference within 20 minutes of the first time they try it.

cards.title: Step 1: Full Morning Protocol
instructions: Oil pulling 5 minutes (new today: see below)
Tongue scraping → Cold water face → 500 ml warm water
cards.whyThisWorks: Oil pulling removes harmful bacteria from the mouth before your first meal. These bacteria travel to your gut with every meal and cause gut inflammation. Removing them at the source takes 5 minutes and works within 4 to 5 days.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: Full morning protocol now takes under 10 minutes

cards.title: How to do oil pulling
paragraphs: Take 1 tablespoon of sesame oil or coconut oil. Swish it gently around your mouth for 5 minutes. Do not gargle. Do not swallow. Keep it moving between your teeth and gums slowly. After 5 minutes: spit into a bin (not the sink, oil blocks drains). Then scrape your tongue. Then rinse with warm water. Then brush teeth normally.

cards.title: Step 2: Warm Compress After Your Biggest Meal
instructions: After your largest meal of the day, do this:
Take a small towel or cloth. Run it under warm water. Wring it out.
Lie down or sit comfortably.
Place the warm cloth on your belly: from your belly button to just below your ribs.
Leave it for 5 minutes. Breathe slowly.
The cloth should feel warm and comfortable: not hot.
cards.whyThisWorks: The digestive organs work best when blood flow to them is high. A warm compress directly increases blood flow, speeds up digestion, and removes the heavy, full feeling after eating. This technique is used in physiotherapy clinics for gut motility problems. It costs nothing and works immediately.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: Immediately: post-meal heaviness reduces within 20 to 30 minutes

cards.title: Step 3: Jeera Water in the Afternoon
instructions: Boil 1 teaspoon of cumin seeds (jeera) in 300 ml of water for 5 minutes.
Strain and drink warm.
This replaces any cold drink or packaged tea in the afternoon.
cards.whyThisWorks: Cumin directly stimulates the pancreas to release more digestive enzymes. Better enzymes mean less undigested food fermenting in the lower gut: which means less gas and less bloating.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: Same day: afternoon bloating and gas reduce noticeably

cards.goal: First exercise day: barefoot grounding and post-meal walks begin

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Exercise enters the program today: but not in the way you expect. The gut microbiome evolved alongside constant contact with the natural world. Modern life indoors has removed most of this contact. Barefoot walking on natural ground restores something important. Post-meal walking activates the gut's natural cleaning system.

cards.title: Step 1: Full Morning Protocol
instructions: Oil pulling 5 min → Tongue scraping → Cold water face → 500 ml warm water
This sequence is now fixed every morning. Do it in this order.
cards.whyThisWorks: Each step prepares for the next. Oil pulling first removes bacteria, scraping collects residue, cold water fires the vagus nerve, warm water triggers the bowel reflex.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: Cumulative: stronger each day

cards.title: Morning: Barefoot Grounding (10 minutes)
paragraphs: Find any patch of natural ground: grass, soil, earth or sand.
Remove your shoes and socks.
Stand or walk slowly for 10 minutes. Morning before 9 AM is ideal.
A garden, park, or any outdoor grassy area works perfectly.
If you cannot go outside: stand on natural stone or tile floor, not carpet.
cards.highlight: The earth carries a mild negative electrical charge. Skin contact allows electrons to enter the body and reduce gut inflammation markers measurably within one week. Soil microorganisms also enter through skin contact and add beneficial bacteria to your gut microbiome: something urban living has almost completely removed.

cards.title: Post-Meal Walk: After Lunch and After Dinner
instructions: Walk at a comfortable, relaxed pace for 10 to 15 minutes after each main meal.
Keep one hand lightly on your belly as you walk.
Breathe through your nose only.
cards.whyThisWorks: Walking activates the migrating motor complex: your gut's natural cleaning wave that sweeps debris through the small intestine. This only works during gentle movement. Post-meal walking reduces blood sugar, reduces post-meal heaviness, and speeds digestion.

cards.title: Step 2: Warm Compress After Biggest Meal
instructions: 5 minutes warm cloth on belly after your largest meal.
Add slow deep breathing during the compress today.
Breathe in through the nose, out through the mouth.
The breathing and the warmth together double the blood flow benefit.
cards.whyThisWorks: Diaphragmatic breathing during the warm compress physically massages the digestive organs from above with every breath.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: Immediate post-meal relief building

cards.goal: Learn what lactose intolerance is and whether it applies to you

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Today adds two new gut practices: abdominal yoga and the deep belly hum. But today also addresses one of the most overlooked causes of gut problems in India: lactose sensitivity. If dairy has been appearing in your pattern map from Day 2, today gives you the science and the practical steps to understand it.

cards.title: What is lactose intolerance: explained simply
paragraphs: Lactose is the natural sugar found in dairy products: milk, curd, paneer, ice cream.
Lactase is the enzyme your body uses to digest lactose.
70 to 80% of South Asian adults naturally produce less lactase as they age. This is not a disease: it is a very common genetic variation in India.
cards.highlight: Track dairy calmly and practically. You do not need to remove everything unless the day asks for it.

cards.title: What happens when lactose is not digested
paragraphs: Undigested lactose travels to the large intestine where gut bacteria ferment it.
This fermentation produces gas, bloating, cramping, and sometimes loose stools.
Symptoms appear 30 minutes to 2 hours after eating dairy.
The symptoms look exactly like general gut problems: which is why many people never realise dairy is the cause.
cards.highlight: Track dairy calmly and practically. You do not need to remove everything unless the day asks for it.

cards.title: The three levels of sensitivity
paragraphs: Mild: can tolerate small amounts. Curd and buttermilk are usually fine.
Moderate: most liquid milk causes symptoms. Fermented dairy is usually okay.
Severe: almost all dairy causes symptoms. Even curd in large amounts.

cards.title: Good news for Indian users
paragraphs: Traditional Indian cooking already favours fermented dairy: curd, buttermilk, chaas, lassi. These have significantly lower lactose because fermentation breaks it down.
You may not need to remove dairy entirely. You may just need to switch from liquid milk to fermented dairy.

cards.title: Pose 1: Knees to Chest (2 minutes)
paragraphs: Lie on your back. Pull both knees to your chest. Rock gently side to side.
This directly compresses the ascending and descending colon. Gas is released.

cards.title: Pose 2: Seated Spinal Twist (1 minute each side)
paragraphs: Sit cross-legged. Place right hand on left knee. Twist gently to the left. Hold 30 seconds. Repeat other side.
Wrings the liver, stomach, and intestines like a cloth.

cards.title: Pose 3: Child's Pose (1 minute)
paragraphs: Kneel, sit back on heels, fold forward, arms extended.
Gently compresses the abdominal cavity.

cards.title: Pose 4: Cat and Cow (1 minute)
paragraphs: On hands and knees. Arch back up (cat). Drop belly down (cow). Alternate slowly 10 to 12 times.

cards.title: Pose 5: Lying Twist (30 seconds each side)
paragraphs: Lie on back. Drop both knees to one side. Look opposite. Hold. Repeat.

cards.goal: Use vibration to stimulate the gut from the inside

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Today introduces the most unexpected practice in the program: using sound vibration to stimulate the gut from the inside. Low-frequency vibration physically stimulates the smooth muscle of the digestive tract. This is the same principle used in clinical ultrasound physiotherapy for gut problems. Today you produce this vibration yourself using your own voice.

cards.title: Step 1: Deep Belly Hum: 5 Minutes
instructions: Lie on your back or sit upright.
Place both hands flat on your belly.
Breathe in slowly through the nose.
As you breathe out: make a deep, low-pitched hum. As low as you comfortably can.
Feel the vibration under your hands on your belly.
Repeat for 5 minutes.
Lower pitch is better: you want to feel the belly vibrating, not just the chest.
cards.whyThisWorks: Low-frequency vibration in the 40 to 100 Hz range stimulates the smooth muscle of the intestines: the same mechanism used in clinical ultrasound therapy for gut motility. Many people feel a warmth or movement in the belly within 2 to 3 minutes.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: Same session: warmth and movement in the belly

cards.goal: Start the circadian eating window and review Week 1

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Week 1 closes today. You have built five daily gut practices: vagus nerve activation, oral clearing, temperature alignment, yoga and movement, and sound vibration. Today adds the most powerful structural change in the program: the circadian eating window. This is not a diet. You eat the same foods. You just eat them within a consistent daily window.

cards.title: Step 1: The 12-Hour Eating Window Starts Today
instructions: Note what time you eat breakfast today.
Your last food or snack must be 12 hours after breakfast.
Example: breakfast at 8 AM means last food at 8 PM.
After the window closes: only warm water, herbal tea, or jeera water.
Do not change your breakfast time. Only move the last meal earlier if needed.
cards.whyThisWorks: Gut bacteria follow a precise 24-hour clock: they are most active in the first 8 to 10 hours after your first meal. Eating outside the active window feeds bacteria when they should be resting: causing fermentation, gas, and disrupted sleep. A consistent 12-hour window stabilises the bacteria clock within 5 to 7 days.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: 5 to 7 days: morning digestion improves, energy increases

cards.goal: Add diaphragmatic breathing as an internal gut massage

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Week 2 begins. All Week 1 practices continue. Today adds diaphragmatic breathing: belly breathing instead of chest breathing. The diaphragm is a dome-shaped muscle that sits directly above your stomach and intestines. Every deep belly breath physically massages these organs from above. Most people never use the diaphragm properly. Five minutes of belly breathing daily is one of the most effective free gut health tools available.

cards.title: Step 1: Diaphragmatic Breathing: 5 Minutes
instructions: Lie on your back. Place one hand on your chest and one on your belly.
Breathe in slowly through the nose: only the belly hand should rise. Chest stays still.
Breathe out slowly through the mouth: belly falls. Chest stays still.
In for 4 counts. Out for 6 counts. Repeat for 5 minutes.
If your chest keeps rising: you are chest breathing. Push the air downward.
The first 2 minutes may feel unusual. By 3 to 4 minutes the belly movement feels natural.
cards.whyThisWorks: The diaphragm directly massages the stomach, liver and intestines with every breath cycle. It also supports the lower oesophageal sphincter: weak belly breathing directly causes acid reflux. 5 minutes of proper belly breathing daily is genuinely therapeutic.

days.dayTitle: The Colon Map and the Dairy Test

cards.dayTitle: The Colon Map and the Dairy Test
cards.goal: Learn the colon massage and start the 5-day dairy elimination test

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Today introduces two important things. The colon massage: a clinical physiotherapy technique for constipation and bloating. And for those who have noted dairy in their pattern map: the 5-day dairy elimination test.

cards.title: Step 1: The Colon Massage: 5 Minutes
instructions: Lie on your back. Use your palm or three fingers of your right hand.

cards.title: Start at the lower right side of your belly: just above the right hip bone.
paragraphs: Move slowly upward along the right side to the bottom of your right ribs.

cards.title: Move across to the left side: below both ribs.
paragraphs: Move slowly down the left side toward the left hip.

cards.title: Continue the stroke toward the centre and downward.
paragraphs: Use firm but gentle circular pressure: like kneading dough lightly. Not painful.
Repeat this full circuit 5 to 6 times slowly. Total time: 5 minutes.
Best done in the morning on an empty stomach.
If any area feels tight: spend an extra 30 seconds on it gently.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: Same session for many people: bowel movement often follows within the hour

cards.title: The 5-Day Dairy Elimination Test: starts today
instructions: If dairy appeared in your pattern map on Days 2 or 5: start this test today.

cards.title: What to remove for 5 days (Days 9 to 14)
paragraphs: Remove: regular milk in all forms: in chai, as a drink, in cooking
Remove: packaged flavoured yogurt, ice cream, kheer, rabri

cards.title: What to KEEP: these are usually fine even with lactose sensitivity
paragraphs: Keep: homemade curd (perugu/dahi): fermentation breaks down 25 to 40% of the lactose
Keep: fresh buttermilk (chaas/majjiga): lowest lactose of all dairy
Keep: small amounts of paneer: test your personal tolerance
cards.highlight: Track dairy calmly and practically. You do not need to remove everything unless the day asks for it.

cards.title: Replace milk in chai with
paragraphs: Black tea or green tea without milk: just for these 5 days
Or use oat milk or almond milk if available

cards.title: What to notice
paragraphs: Does bloating reduce? Does post-meal discomfort reduce? Does gas reduce? Write down what changes.
Results become clear by Days 12 to 14.

cards.goal: Address emotional tension stored in the gut

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: The gut has more nerve cells than the spinal cord. Unprocessed stress and emotion physically store in the gut as muscular tension. Today you identify and release this tension directly. This is not about therapy or analysis. It is a simple physical practice of noticing where the gut holds tension and breathing into it.

cards.title: Step 1: Gut Tension Mapping: 5 Minutes
instructions: Lie on your back. Close your eyes.
Slowly scan from your throat down to your lower belly.
Look for any areas that feel tight, hard, or uncomfortable.
Place one hand on each tight area for 30 seconds.
Breathe slowly: imagine the breath reaching that tight area directly.
Do not try to fix it. Just notice it and breathe.
If you feel a gurgle, a release, or a sigh: that is the gut letting go.
cards.whyThisWorks: The gut stores stress in the same way a tense muscle holds a knot. The act of consciously breathing into a tight spot triggers the parasympathetic nervous system to begin releasing that tension.

cards.dayTitle: The Emotional Gut and Lactose Management
cards.goal: Address the mind-gut connection and build long-term lactose management

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Two things matter today. The emotional gut: addressing stress as a physical gut disruptor. And for those with lactose sensitivity: a clear, practical, permanent management plan using Indian foods.

cards.title: Step 1: Before-Meal Breathing Practice
instructions: Before each main meal today: sit down and take 5 slow belly breaths.
In for 4 counts. Out for 6 counts.
Let your shoulders drop. Let your belly relax.
Then eat.
Do this before every meal for the rest of the program.
cards.whyThisWorks: Stress hormones constrict the stomach and reduce enzyme production by up to 50%. Two minutes of belly breathing before eating switches the nervous system from fight-or-flight to rest-and-digest. Food eaten in a calm state is absorbed significantly better than food eaten in a hurry.
cards.highlight: You may feel this in: Same day: post-meal bloating reduces when meals are eaten calmly

cards.title: For chai: the most common daily dairy habit
paragraphs: Reduce the milk in your chai gradually over 2 weeks.
Use less than you currently do: the taste adjusts quickly.
Alternatively: make chai with oat milk: available in most Indian supermarkets now.
Or switch to black tea or green tea on 3 to 4 days per week.

cards.title: For curd-based dishes: these are good for you
paragraphs: Raita, curd rice, kadhi, lassi: all are fermented dairy and generally fine.
Most lactose-sensitive people tolerate curd and buttermilk very well.
These are better for your gut than regular milk in every way.

cards.title: For social occasions and celebrations
paragraphs: You do not need to announce lactose sensitivity to anyone.
Simply avoid mithai, ice cream, and kheer at the end of meals.
Nobody notices and you save yourself days of discomfort.

cards.title: The lactase enzyme supplement option
paragraphs: Lactase enzyme tablets (available at Indian pharmacies: ask for Lactaid or similar)
allow you to eat any dairy without symptoms.
Useful for weddings, festivals, and occasions where avoiding dairy is impractical.
Take one tablet just before eating dairy-heavy food.

cards.title: The most important thing to remember
paragraphs: Lactose sensitivity is not an illness. It is information about your body.
Traditional Indian cooking already favours fermented dairy: curd, buttermilk, chaas.
You are moving toward the traditional diet your body was always better suited to.
